Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Corrections and Conditional Release Act and to make related and consequential amendments to other Acts (life sentences)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code and the Corrections and Conditional Release Act and to make related and consequential amendments ...

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to make a life sentence of imprisonment without eligibility for parole mandatory for high treason and for planned and deliberate murders that are referred to in subsection 231(4), (5) or (6.01) of that Act or in which the accused芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, is of such a brutal nature as to compel the conclusion that the accused芒聙聶s behaviour in the future is unlikely to be inhibited by normal standards of behavioural restraint.

The enactment also amends the Criminal Code to give a judge the discretion to impose a life sentence of imprisonment without eligibility for parole for any other first degree murder and for any second degree murder if the accused was previously convicted either of murder or of an offence referred to in section 4 or 6 of the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act that had as its basis an intentional killing. The enactment provides that the court芒聙聶s decision is to be based on the accused芒聙聶s age and character, the nature of the offence, the circumstances surrounding its commission and any jury recommendation.

The enactment also amends the Corrections and Conditional Release Act to allow an offender who is sentenced to life without parole to apply for executive release after serving 35 years of their sentence. Executive release is granted or denied by the Governor in Council.
Finally, the enactment makes related and consequential amendments to the National Defence Act, the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act and the International Transfer of Offenders Act.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Life Means Life Act.
Paragraph (b) of the definition sentence in section 673 of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under subsection 109(1) or 110(1), section 161, subsection 164.2(1) or 194(1), section 259, 261 or 462.37, subsection 491.1(2), 730(1) or 737(3) or section 738, 739, 742.1, 742.3, 743.6, 745.31, 745.4 or 745.5,

Section.Text: Section 675 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.2):

Subsection.Text: A person who has been convicted of a murder described in paragraph 744.1(1)(b) and sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole may appeal to the court of appeal against the finding that the murder is one described in any of subparagraphs 744.1(1)(b)(i) to (iv).
A person against whom an order under section 745.31 has been made may appeal to the court of appeal against the order.

Section.Text: Subsection 676(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Appeal relating to eligibility for parole
Subsection.Text: The Attorney General or counsel instructed by the Attorney General for the purpose may appeal to the court of appeal against the finding that the murder is not one described in any of subparagraphs 744.1(1)(b)(i) to (iv).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Appeal against decision not to make order芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣section 745.31 or subsection 745.51(1)
Subsection.Text: The Attorney General or counsel instructed by the Attorney General for the purpose may appeal to the court of appeal against the decision of the court not to make an order under section 745.31 or subsection 745.51(1).

Section.Text: Subsection 727(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: This section does not apply to a person referred to in paragraph 745(b) or (b.1).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 745:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 745.1, an accused who is convicted of any of the following offences shall be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: high treason; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: murder that is planned and deliberate

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the victim is described in any of paragraphs 231(4)(a) to (c),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the death is caused by the accused while committing or attempting to commit an offence under one of the sections referred to in any of paragraphs 231(5)(a) to (f),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the death is caused by the accused while committing or attempting to commit an indictable offence under this or any other Act of Parliament where the act or omission constituting the offence also constitutes a terrorist activity, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the accused芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, is of such a brutal nature as to compel the conclusion that the accused芒聙聶s behaviour in the future is unlikely to be inhibited by normal stand脗颅ards of behavioural restraint.

Subsection.Text: A prosecutor who intends to apply to have an accused sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole for a murder described in paragraph (1)(b) shall provide the accused with notice of their intention to do so before the accused enters a plea.

For greater certainty, for the purposes of subparagraph (1)(b)(iv), if the jury specifies under paragraph 745.001(e) that the accused芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, is of a brutal nature, the judge shall determine if the accused芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, is of such a brutal nature as to compel the conclusion that the accused芒聙聶s behaviour in the future is unlikely to be inhibited by normal standards of behavioural restraint.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 745(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a person who has been convicted of first degree murder other than first degree murder described in paragraph 744.1(1)(b), that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served 25 years of the sentence, unless the judge referred to in section 745.31 orders under that section that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a person who has been convicted of second degree murder and who has previously been convicted of culpable homicide that is murder, however described in this Act, that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served 25 years of the sentence, unless the judge referred to in section 745.31 orders under that section that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a person who has been convicted of second degree murder and who has previously been convicted of an offence under section 4 or 6 of the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act that had as its basis an intentional killing, whether or not it was planned and deliberate, that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served 25 years of the sentence, unless the judge referred to in section 745.31 orders under that section that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a person who has been convicted of second degree murder other than second degree murder described in paragraph (b) or (b.1), that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served at least 10 years of the sentence or any greater number of years, not being more than 25, that has been substituted under section 745.4; and

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 745:

If an accused is charged with murder and is provided with notice under subsection 744.1(2) or 745.31(2), the judge presiding at the trial of the accused shall, before the jury retires to consider its verdict, instruct the jury that, if they find the accused guilty of the murder, they must specify to the judge, when they render their verdict,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the murder was planned and deliberate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the victim was a person described in any of paragraphs 231(4)(a) to (c), whether the victim was such a person;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the death was caused by the accused while committing or attempting to commit an offence under one of the sections referred to in any of paragraphs 231(5)(a) to (f), whether the death was so caused;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the death was caused by the accused while committing or attempting to commit an indictable offence under this or any other Act of Parliament where the act or omission constituting the offence also constituted a terrorist activity, whether the death was so caused; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if the murder was planned and deliberate and evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the accused芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, was of a brutal nature, whether the behaviour was of a brutal nature.

Section.Text: Section 745.2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to section 745.3, if a jury finds an accused guilty of second degree murder, other than second degree murder described in paragraph 745(b) or (b.1), the judge presiding at the trial shall, before discharging the jury, put to them the following question:
Section.Oath: You have found the accused guilty of second degree murder and the law requires that I now pronounce a sentence of imprisonment for life against the accused. Do you wish to make any recommendation with respect to the number of years that the accused must serve before the accused is eligible for release on parole? You are not required to make any recommendation but if you do, your recommendation will be considered by me when I am determining whether I should substitute for the 10-year period, which the law would otherwise require the accused to serve before the accused is eligible to be considered for release on parole, a number of years that is more than 10 but not more than 25.

Section.Text: Section 745.21 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of an accused who has been sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole for a murder for which they were previously convicted.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 745.3:

Section.MarginalNote: Order芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣no eligibility for parole

Subsection.Text: At the time of sentencing under section 745 of an accused who is convicted of a first degree murder described in paragraph 745(a) or of a second degree murder described in paragraph 745(b) or (b.1), the judge who presided at the trial of the accused or, if that judge is unable to do so, any judge of the same court, may on application of the prosecutor and having regard to the age and character of the accused, the nature of the offence and the circumstances surrounding its commission, and to any recommendation made under section 745.32, by order, decide that the accused is to serve a sentence of imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole.
A prosecutor who intends to make an application referred to in subsection (1) shall provide the accused with notice of their intention to do so before the accused enters a plea.

Section.Text: If a jury finds an accused guilty of first degree murder described in paragraph 745(a) or of second degree murder described in paragraph 745(b) or (b.1), the judge who presided at the trial shall, before discharging the jury, put to them the following question:
Section.Oath: You have found the accused guilty of first degree murder (or second degree murder) and the law requires that I now pronounce a sentence of imprisonment for life against the accused. Do you wish to make any recommendation with respect to whether the accused should be sentenced to life imprisonment without eligibility for parole? You are not required to make a recommendation but if you do, I will consider your recommendation.

Section.Text: Section 745.4 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Order芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣period of ineligibility for parole
Section.Text: Subject to section 745.5, at the time of the sentencing under section 745 of an accused who is convicted of second degree murder, other than second degree murder described in paragraph 745(b) or (b.1), the judge who presided at the trial of the accused or, if that judge is unable to do so, any judge of the same court, may, having regard to the character of the accused, the nature of the offence and the circumstances surrounding its commission, and to any recommendation made under section 745.2, by order, substitute for 10 years a number of years of imprisonment (being more than 10 but not more than 25) without eligibility for parole, as the judge considers fit in the circumstances.
Section 745.51 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of an offender who has been sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole for a murder for which they were previously convicted.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 746.1:
In respect of an offender who has been sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no absence without escort may be authorized under the Corrections and Conditional Release Act or the Prisons and Reformatories Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: no absence with escort may be authorized under those Acts for any reason other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: medical reasons or in order to attend judicial proceedings or a coroner芒聙聶s inquest, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: administrative or compassionate reasons, community service, personal development for rehabilitative purposes or family contact, including parental responsibilities, on condition that the absence is approved by the Parole Board of Canada and that the offender has served 35 years of their sentence.

Section.Text: Subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition offender in subsection 2(1) of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: by reason of parole, statutory release or executive release granted under section 156.11,

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 17(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The institutional head may, subject to sections 746.1 and 747 of the Criminal Code, subsection 226.1(2) of the National Defence Act and subsection 15(2) of the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act, author-ize the temporary absence of an inmate, other than an inmate described in subsection 17.1(1), if the inmate is escorted by a staff member or other person authorized by the institutional head and, in the opinion of the institutional head,

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 156:
The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: Board芒聙聜means the Parole Board of Canada referred to in section 103.
Commissioner芒聙聜has the same meaning as in Part I.
institutional head芒聙聜has the same meaning as in Part I.
Minister芒聙聜has the same meaning as in Part I.
offender芒聙聜means a person on whom a sentence is imposed.
parole supervisor芒聙聜has the same meaning as in Part II.
penitentiary芒聙聜has the same meaning as in Part I.

sentence芒聙聜means a sentence of imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole and includes such a sentence that is imposed by a foreign entity on a Canadian who has been transferred to Canada under the International Transfer of Offenders Act.
Service芒聙聜has the same meaning as in Part I.
victim芒聙聜has the same meaning as in Part I.

Subsection.Text: An offender may make an application in writing to the Minister for executive release after the offender has served 35 years of their sentence.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Power to direct assessment by Board
Subsection.Text: On the receipt of an application the Minister may direct the Board to assess the offender芒聙聶s case under section 156.03.
If the Minister directs an assessment of the offender芒聙聶s case, the Board shall, within the prescribed period, assess whether

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the offender will, by reoffending, present an undue risk to society; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the release of the offender will contribute to the protection of society by facilitating the successful reintegration of the offender into society as a law-abiding citizen.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Service to provide information to Board
Subsection.Text: The Service shall provide the Board with all the information under its control that is relevant to the Board芒聙聶s assessment of the offender芒聙聶s case.

At the Board芒聙聶s request, the Service shall take all reasonable steps to provide the Board with any additional information under its control that is relevant to its assessment of the offender芒聙聶s case.

In the period prescribed for assessing the offender芒聙聶s case, the Board shall provide to the offender, in writing, in whichever official language the offender requests, the information that is to be considered in the assessment or a summary of it and shall notify the Service that it has done so.

An offender who does not have an adequate understanding of either official language is entitled to the as-sistance of an interpreter for the purpose of understanding the information provided to the offender under subsection (1).
The Board may withhold from the offender as much information as is strictly necessary to protect the interest identified in paragraph (a) or (b) if the Board has reasonable grounds to believe

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that any information should not be disclosed on the grounds of public interest; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that its disclosure would jeopardize

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the safety of any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the security of a penitentiary or provincial correctional facility, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the conduct of any lawful investigation.

Section.Text: In assessing the offender芒聙聶s case, the Board shall provide the offender with a reasonable opportunity to make representations in writing.

Subsection.Text: On the completion of its assessment of the offender芒聙聶s case, the Board shall prepare a report on the assessment and provide it to the Minister.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Copy of report to offender and Service
Subsection.Text: The Board shall provide a copy of the report to the offender, in whichever official language the offender requests, and to the Service.

The Minister shall review the offender芒聙聶s application for executive release on receipt of the Board芒聙聶s report on the assessment or, if the Minister has not, under subsection 156.02(2), directed the Board to assess the offender芒聙聶s case, on receipt of the offender芒聙聶s application.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Service to provide Minister with information
Subsection.Text: The Service shall provide the Minister with the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the Minister directs the Board to assess the offender芒聙聶s case, the information that the Service provided the Board with under subsection 156.03(2) or (3) and any other information under its control that is relevant to the Minister芒聙聶s review of the application; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the Minister does not direct the Board to assess the offender芒聙聶s case, the information that the Service would have provided the Board with under subsection 156.03(2) if the Minister had directed the Board to assess the offender芒聙聶s case and any other information under its control that is relevant to the Minister芒聙聶s review of the application.

Subsection.Text: In reviewing the application, the Minister shall assess whether the fundamental purpose and the objectives of sentencing have been met by the portion of the sentence that the offender has served, taking into account the following criteria:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the character of the offender;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the offender芒聙聶s conduct while serving the sentence;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whether the offender has accepted responsibility for the offence for which they have been convicted, including by acknowledging the harm done to victims and to the community;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the nature of the offence for which the offender was convicted and the circumstances surrounding its commission; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any statement presented by the victim at the time of sentencing and any statements presented by the victim or a person referred to in subsection 156.12(3) to the Minister with respect to the application.

Subsection.Text: The Minister shall also assess if there are humanitarian or compassionate reasons for granting executive release, including the following reasons:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the offender is terminally ill;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the offender芒聙聶s physical or mental health is likely to suffer serious damage if the offender continues to be held in confinement; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the offender is one for whom continued confinement would constitute an excessive hardship that was not reasonably foreseeable at the time the offender was sentenced.

Section.MarginalNote: Service to provide offender with information

Subsection.Text: When the Service provides the Minister with information under subsection 156.07(2), it shall provide the offender with that information, or a summary of it芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣other than the information that the Board provided the offender with under section 156.04芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣in writing, in whichever official language the offender requests.

An offender who does not have an adequate understanding of either official language is entitled to the as-sistance of an interpreter for the purpose of understanding the information provided to the offender under subsection (1).

The Service may withhold from the offender as much information as is strictly necessary to protect the interest identified in any of paragraphs (a) to (c) if it has reasonable grounds to believe the disclosure of the information would jeopardize

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the safety of any person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the security of a penitentiary or provincial correctional facility; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the conduct of any lawful investigation.

Section.Text: In reviewing the application, the Minister shall provide

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the offender with a reasonable opportunity to make representations in writing;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the victim with a reasonable opportunity to present a statement describing the harm, property damage or loss suffered by them as a result of the commission of the offence and its continuing impact on them芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣including any safety concerns芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣and commenting on the possible release of the offender; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any person referred to in subsection 156.12(3) with a reasonable opportunity to present a statement describing the harm, property damage or loss suffered by them as a result of any act of the offender in respect of which a complaint was made to the police or Crown attorney or an information was laid under the Criminal Code, and its continuing impact on them芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣including any safety concerns芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣and commenting on the possible release of the offender.

Subsection.Text: On the completion of the Minister芒聙聶s review of the offender芒聙聶s application for executive release, the Minister shall prepare a report on the review.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Application referred to Governor in Council
Subsection.Text: Once the report is completed, the Minister shall refer the application to the Governor in Council for a decision and shall include with the application his or her report, the representations made by the offender and any statements presented by the victim or a person referred to in subsection 156.12(3).

The Governor in Council shall, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, grant the executive release or deny the offender芒聙聶s application for executive release that was referred to it by the Minister.
The Minister shall provide a copy of the order to the offender.
If the Governor in Council denies the offender芒聙聶s application, the offender may re-apply for executive release after the expiry of five years after the day on which the order was made.

Heading.TitleText: Disclosure of Information to Victims and Other Persons

Subsection.Text: At the request of a victim of an offence committed by an offender, the Chairperson of the Board or the Commissioner, as the case may be,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shall disclose to the victim the following information about the offender:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that the Minister is to review the offender芒聙聶s application for executive release under section 156.07, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the date of any hearing in respect of the suspension, termination or revocation of the offender芒聙聶s executive release under section 156.21; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may disclose to the victim any of the following information about the offender if, in the opinion of the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, the interest of the victim in the disclosure clearly outweighs any invasion of the offender芒聙聶s privacy that could result from the disclosure:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the date, if any, on which the offender is to be released on escorted temporary absence or on executive release,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any of the conditions attached to an offender芒聙聶s executive release or escorted temporary absence and the reasons for the escorted temporary absence, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the destination of the offender when released on executive release and whether the offender will be in the vicinity of the victim while travelling to that destination.

Subsection.Text: The Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, shall disclose to the victim the information referred to in paragraph (1)(c) before the day on which the offender is released and, unless it is not feasible to do so, the Commissioner shall disclose it at least 14 days before that day.
The Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, shall disclose to the victim any changes to the information referred to in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) that he or she disclosed.

If an offender has been transferred from a penitentiary to a provincial correctional facility, the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, may, at the request of a victim, disclose to the victim the name of the province in which the provincial facility is located if, in the opinion of the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, the interest of the victim in the disclosure clearly outweighs any invasion of the offender芒聙聶s privacy that could result from the disclosure.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure of information to victim芒聙聣芒聙聰 removal
Subsection.Text: At the request of a victim, the Commissioner may disclose to the victim that the offender has been removed from Canada under the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act if, in the Commissioner芒聙聶s opinion, the interest of the victim in the disclosure clearly outweighs any invasion of the offender芒聙聶s privacy that could result from the disclosure.

At the request of a victim, the Commissioner shall provide the victim with access to a photograph of the offender taken when the offender is released on executive release芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣and any subsequent photograph of the offender taken by the Service芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣if, in the Commissioner芒聙聶s opinion, to do so would not have a negative impact on the safety of the public.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure of information to other persons
Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (2.2) also apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to a person who satisfies the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the person suffered physical or emotional harm, property damage or economic loss, as the result of an act of an offender, whether or not the offender was prosecuted or convicted for that act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that a complaint was made to the police or the Crown attorney, or an information was laid under the Criminal Code, in respect of that act.

Subsection.Text: A victim may designate a representative to whom the information referred to in subsections (1) to (2.2) is to be disclosed on the victim芒聙聶s behalf. In that case, the victim shall provide the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, with the representative芒聙聶s contact information.

A victim who has made a request referred to in subsection (1), (2), (2.1) or (2.2) may inform the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, in writing that they no longer want the information to be disclosed to them. In that case, the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, shall not contact them or their representative, if any, unless the victim subsequently makes the request again.

The Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, may consider a victim to have withdrawn a request referred to in subsection (1), (2), (2.1) or (2.2) if he or she has made reasonable efforts to contact the victim and has failed to do so.

Subsections (3.1) to (3.3) also apply, with any necessary modifications, to a person who has satisfied the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, of the matters referred to in paragraphs (3)(a) and (b).
In this section, Chairperson includes a person or class of persons designated, by name or by position, by the Chairperson.

Section.Text: For the purposes of this Part and subject to the Prisons and Reformatories Act, the International Transfer of Offenders Act, the National Defence Act, the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act and the Criminal Code, the Board has exclusive jurisdiction and absolute discretion

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to relieve the offender from compliance with any condition referred to in subsection 156.14(1) or vary the application to the offender of any such condition;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to impose, remove or vary any condition referred to in subsection 156.14(2) or (3);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to terminate or revoke an offender芒聙聶s executive release, whether or not the offender is in custody under a warrant of apprehension issued as a result of a suspension of the executive release;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to cancel the termination or revocation of an offender芒聙聶s executive release; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: to approve an absence with escort for the reasons referred to in subparagraph 747(b)(ii) of the Criminal Code.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (5), every offender who is granted executive release under section 156.11 is subject to the conditions prescribed by subsection 161(1) of the Corrections and Conditional Release Regulations, with any modifications that the circumstances require.

The Board may impose any conditions on the executive release of an offender that it considers reasonable and necessary to protect society and to facilitate the offender芒聙聶s successful reintegration into society. For greater certainty, the conditions may include any condition regarding the offender芒聙聶s use of drugs or alcohol, including in cases when that use has been identified as a risk factor in the offender芒聙聶s criminal behaviour.
If, in the opinion of the Board, the circumstances justify it, the Board may require an offender, as a condition of the executive release, to reside in a community residential facility.

In subsection (3), community residential facility means a place that provides accommodation to an offender who is on executive release and includes a community correctional centre but does not include any other penitentiary.

A condition imposed under subsection (3) that an offender reside in a community correctional centre is valid only if consented to in writing by the Commissioner or a person designated, by name or by position, by the Commissioner.
A condition imposed under subsection (2) or (3) is valid for the period that the Board specifies.
The Board may, in accordance with the regulations, before an offender is released on executive release,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relieve the offender from compliance with any condition referred to in subsection (1) or vary the application to the offender of any such condition; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: remove or vary a condition imposed under subsection (2) or (3).

Section.Text: An offender who has been granted executive release shall comply with any instructions given by a Board member or a person designated, by name or by position, by the Chairperson of the Board or the Commissioner, or given by the institutional head, or by the offender芒聙聶s parole supervisor, respecting any conditions of the release in order to prevent a breach of any such condition or to protect society.

Heading.TitleText: Suspension, Termination or Revocation of Executive Release

Subsection.Text: A Board member, or a person designated by name or by position by the Chairperson of the Board or the Commissioner, may, when an offender breaches a condition of their executive release or when the member or person is satisfied that it is necessary and reasonable to suspend the executive release in order to prevent a breach of any condition of that release or to protect society, by warrant,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspend the release;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the apprehension of the offend脗颅er; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: authorize the recommitment of the offend脗颅er to custody until the suspension is cancelled or the release is terminated or revoked.

Subsection.Text: A person designated under subsection (1) may, by warrant, order the transfer to a penitentiary of the offender who is recommitted to custody under subsection (1) in a place other than a penitentiary.

The person who signs a warrant referred to in subsection (1) or any other person designated under that subsection shall, immediately after the recommitment of the offender, review the offender芒聙聶s case and within 30 days after the recommitment or any shorter period that the Board directs, cancel the suspension or refer the case to the Board together with an assessment of the case stating the conditions, if any, under which the offender could in that person芒聙聶s opinion reasonably resume their executive release.

The Board shall, on the referral to it of the offender芒聙聶s case, review the case and, within the prescribed period, unless at the offender芒聙聶s request the review is adjourned by the Board or is postponed by a Board member or by a person designated by the Chairperson by name or position,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the Board is satisfied that the offender will, by reoffending, present an undue risk to society,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: terminate the executive release if the undue risk is due to circumstances beyond the offender芒聙聶s control, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: revoke it in any other case; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the Board is not so satisfied, cancel the suspension.

Subsection.Text: If, in the Board芒聙聶s opinion, it is necessary and reasonable to do so to protect society or to facilitate the successful reintegration of the offender into society, the Board, when it cancels a suspension of the offender芒聙聶s executive release, may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: reprimand the offender in order to warn the offender of the Board芒聙聶s dissatisfaction with the offender芒聙聶s behaviour since executive release;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vary the conditions of executive release; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: order that the cancellation is not to take effect until the expiry of a specified period of not more than 30 days after the day on which the Board makes its decision, if the offender芒聙聶s failure to comply with a condition of the executive release constitutes at least the second such failure that led to a suspension of the release during the offender芒聙聶s sentence.

Subsection.Text: If a person referred to in subsection (3) or the Board cancels a suspension under this section, the person or the Board, as the case may be, shall forward a notification of the cancellation of the suspension or an electron脗颅ically transmitted copy of the notification to the person in charge of the facility in which the offender is being held.

Independently of subsections (1) to (6), if the Board is satisfied that the continued executive release of an offender would constitute an undue risk to society by reason of the offender reoffending, the Board may, at any time,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: terminate the executive release, if the undue risk is due to circumstances beyond the offender芒聙聶s control; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: revoke it in any other case.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Confirmation or cancellation of Board芒聙聶s decision
Subsection.Text: The Board shall, within the prescribed period, either confirm or cancel its decision under subsection (7).

Section.Text: A Board member, or a person designated by name or position by the Chairperson of the Board or the Commissioner, may, by warrant, authorize an offender芒聙聶s apprehension and recommitment to custody if the offender芒聙聶s executive release is terminated or revoked under subsection 156.16(7).

Subsection.Text: A warrant of apprehension issued under section 156.16 or 156.17, or an electron脗颅ically transmitted copy of such a warrant, shall be executed by any peace officer to whom it is given in any place in Canada as if it had been originally issued or subsequently endorsed by a justice or other lawful authority having jurisdiction in that place.
A peace officer who believes on reasonable grounds that a warrant is in force under this Part for the apprehension of a person may arrest the person without warrant and remand the person in custody.

If a person is arrested under subsection (2), the warrant of apprehension, or an electron脗颅ically transmitted copy of it, shall be executed within 48 hours after the arrest is made, failing which the person shall be released.

Section.MarginalNote: Arrest without warrant芒聙聣芒聙聰 breach of conditions
Section.Text: A peace officer may arrest without warrant an offender who has committed a breach of a condition of their executive release, or whom the peace officer finds committing such a breach, unless the peace officer

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: believes on reasonable grounds that the public interest may be satisfied without arresting the person, having regard to all the circumstances, including the need to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: establish the identity of the person, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: prevent the continuation or repetition of the breach; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: believes on reasonable grounds that the person will not fail to report to their parole supervisor to be dealt with according to law if the peace officer does not arrest the person.

Subsection.Text: If an offender芒聙聶s executive release is terminated or revoked, the offender shall be recommitted to custody and shall continue to serve their sentence.

An offender whose executive release has been terminated or revoked may make a new application for executive release under section 156.02 after the expiry of one year after the day on which the offender芒聙聶s executive release is terminated or of five years after the day on which it is revoked, as the case may be.

The Board may review an offend脗颅er芒聙聶s case in respect of the suspension, termination or revocation of the offender芒聙聶s executive release by way of a hearing conducted in whichever official language the offender requests.

Subject to subsections (3) and (3.1), the Board or a person designated, by name or by position, by the Chairperson of the Board shall, subject to any conditions that the Board or person considers appropriate and after taking into account the offender芒聙聶s views, permit a person who applies in writing to attend as an observer at a hearing relating to an offender, unless the Board or person is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the hearing is likely to be disrupted or the ability of the Board to consider the matter before it is likely to be adversely affected by the presence of that person or of that person in conjunction with other persons who have applied to attend the hearing;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person芒聙聶s presence is likely to adversely affect those who have provided information to the Board, including victims, members of a victim芒聙聶s family or members of the offender芒聙聶s family;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person芒聙聶s presence is likely to adversely affect an appropriate balance between that person芒聙聶s or the public芒聙聶s interest in knowing and the public芒聙聶s interest in the successful reintegration of the offender into society; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the security and good order of the institution in which the hearing is to be held is likely to be adversely affected by the person芒聙聶s presence.

Subsection.Text: If in the course of a hearing the Board concludes that any of the situations described in subsection (2) exist, it may decide to continue the hearing in the absence of observers or of a particular observer.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Attendance by victim or member of their family
Subsection.Text: In determining whether to permit a victim or a member of the victim芒聙聶s family to attend as an observer at a hearing, the Board or its designate shall make every effort to fully understand the need of the victim and of the members of his or her family to attend the hearing and witness its proceedings. The Board or its designate shall permit a victim or a member of his or her family to attend as an observer unless satisfied that the presence of the victim or family member would result in a situation described in any of paragraphs (2)(a) to (d).

If the Board or its designate decides under subsection (3.1) to not permit a victim or a member of his or her family to attend a hearing, the Board shall provide for the victim or family member to observe the hearing by any means that the Board considers appropriate.

The Board shall permit the offender, in the course of a hearing, to be assisted by a person of the offender芒聙聶s choice unless the Board would not permit the presence of that person as an observer under subsection (2).
A person referred to in subsection (4) is entitled

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to be present at the hearing at all times when the offender is present;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to advise the offender throughout the hearing; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to address, on behalf of the offender, the members of the Board conducting the hearing at times they consider to be conducive to the effective conduct of the hearing.

Subsection.Text: An offender who does not have an adequate understanding of either official language is entitled to the as-sistance of an interpreter at the hearing and for the purpose of understanding any information provided to the offender under section 156.22.
If they are attending a hearing as an observer,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a victim may present a statement describing the harm, property damage or loss suffered by them as a result of the commission of the offence and its continuing impact on them芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣including any safety concerns 芒聙聰芒聙聣and commenting on the possible release of the offender; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person referred to in subsection 156.12(3) may present a statement describing the harm, property damage or loss suffered by them as a result of any act of the offender in respect of which a complaint was made to the police or Crown attorney or an information was laid under the Criminal Code, and its continuing impact on them芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣including any safety concerns芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣and commenting on the possible release of the offender.

Subsection.Text: The Board shall, in deciding whether the offender should resume their executive release and what conditions might be applicable to the release, take into consideration any statement that has been presented in accord-ance with paragraph (7)(a) or (b).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Statements presented in absence of person
Subsection.Text: If a victim or a person referred to in subsection 156.12(3) is not attending a hearing, their statement may be presented at the hearing in the form of a written statement, which may be accompanied by an audio or video recording, or in any other prescribed form.
A victim or a person referred to in subsection 156.12(3) shall, before the hearing, deliver to the Board a transcript of the statement that they plan to present under subsection (7) or (8).

If an observer has been present during a hearing, any information or documents discussed or referred to during the hearing shall not for that reason alone be considered to be publicly available for purposes of the Access to Information Act or the Privacy Act.

At least 15 days before the day set for the review of the offender芒聙聶s case in respect of the suspension, termination or revocation of the offender芒聙聶s executive release, the Board shall provide or cause to be provided to the offender, in writing, in whichever official language the offender requests, the information that is to be considered in the review of the case or a summary of it.

If information referred to in subsection (1) comes into the Board芒聙聶s possession in the 15 days before the day set for the hearing, it shall provide the information or a summary of it to the offender as soon as possible.

An offender may waive the right to be provided with the information or summary or to have it provided within the period referred to in subsection (1). If they waive the latter right and they receive information so late that it is not possible for them to prepare for the review, they are entitled to a postponement and a member of the Board or a person designated by name or position by the Chairperson of the Board shall, at the offender芒聙聶s request, postpone the review for the period that the member or person determines. If the Board receives information so late that it is not possible for it to prepare for the review, a member of the Board or a person designated by name or position by the Chairperson of the Board may postpone the review for any reasonable period that the member or person determines.
The Board may withhold from the offender as much information as is strictly necessary to protect the interest identified in paragraph (a) or (b) if the Board has reasonable grounds to believe

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that any information should not be disclosed on the grounds of public interest; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that its disclosure would jeopardize

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the safety of any person,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the security of a penitentiary or provincial correctional facility, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the conduct of any lawful investigation.

Heading.TitleText: Timing and Effect of Executive Release

Section.Text: If an offender is granted executive release, the release shall take effect, and the offender shall be released, as soon as possible after any period that is necessary to implement the decision to grant the release.

Section.MarginalNote: Police to be notified of release
Section.Text: Before the release of an offender on executive release, the Service shall notify all police forces that have jurisdiction at the destination of the offender if that destination is known, and it shall give the police, and any body authorized by the Service to supervise offenders, all information under its control that is relevant to the supervision of the offender.
Subsections 128(1) to (3) apply, with any necessary modifications, to an offender who has been granted executive release under section 156.11.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 4(h), subparagraph 15.1(1)(b)(i), section 55, subsections 57.1(1) and 66(1), section 84 and subsections 94(1) and (3) apply, with any necessary modifications, in respect of executive release.
Sections 100 to 101, 111 and 143 to 145 apply to this Part with any necessary modifications.

Section.Text: In calculating the period of imprisonment served for the purposes of the Criminal Code, the International Transfer of Offenders Act, the National Defence Act, the Prisons and Reformatories Act and this Act, there shall be included any time spent in custody during the period beginning on the day on which the person was arrested and taken into custody and ending on the day on which the sentence was imposed.
The Governor in Council may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting applications referred to in section 156.02;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: authorizing an employee of the Service or classes of employees of the Service to exercise powers or perform duties or functions that this Part assigns to the Commissioner or the institutional head;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the manner in which requests to the Chairperson or Commissioner are to be made under section 156.12 and how those requests are to be dealt with;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing or otherwise providing for anything that by this Part is to be provided for by regulation; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: generally, for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Part.

Section.Text: The National Defence Act is amended by adding the following before section 226.1:

Section.MarginalNote: Imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 745.1 of the Criminal Code, an accused person who is convicted of any of the following offences shall be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an offence of misconduct in the presence of an enemy (section 73 or 74), an offence related to security (section 75) or an offence in relation to prisoners of war (section 76), if the accused person acted traitorously;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: high treason; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: murder that is planned and deliberate

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the victim is described in any of paragraphs 231(4)(a) to (c) of the Criminal Code,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the death is caused by the accused person while committing or attempting to commit an offence under one of the sections referred to in any of paragraphs 231(5)(a) to (f) of the Criminal Code,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the death is caused by the accused person while committing or attempting to commit a serious offence under this Act or an indictable offence under any other Act of Parliament where the act or omission constituting the offence also constitutes a terrorist activity,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: in which the accused person芒聙聶s behav脗颅iour, associated with the offence, is of such a brutal nature as to compel the conclusion that the accused person芒聙聶s behaviour in the future is unlikely to be inhibited by normal standards of behav脗颅ioural restraint.

Subsection.Text: A prosecutor who intends to apply to have an accused person sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole for a murder described in paragraph (1)(c) shall provide the accused person with notice of their intention to do so before the accused person enters a plea.

For greater certainty, for the purposes of subparagraph (1)(c)(iv), if the panel of a General Court Martial specifies under paragraph 226.11(e) that the accused person芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, is of a brutal nature, the military judge shall determine if the accused person芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, is of such a brutal nature as to compel the conclusion that the accused person芒聙聶s behaviour in the future is unlikely to be inhibited by normal standards of behavioural restraint.
Paragraphs 226.1(1)(a) to (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person who has been convicted of first degree murder other than first degree murder described in paragraph 226.01(1)(c), imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served 25 years of the sentence, unless the military judge orders that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole under section 745.31 of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person who has been convicted of second degree murder and has previously been convicted of culpable homicide that is murder, imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served 25 years of the sentence, unless the military judge orders that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole under section 745.31 of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person who has been convicted of second degree murder and who has previously been convicted of an offence under section 4 or 6 of the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act that had as its basis an intentional killing, whether or not it was planned and deliberate, imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served 25 years of the sentence, unless the military judge orders that the person be sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole under section 745.31 of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a person who has been convicted of second degree murder other than second degree murder described in paragraph (b) or (c), imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole until the person has served at least 10 years of the sentence or any greater number of years, not being more than 25, that has been substituted under subsection (2); or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 226.1(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
Sections 745.1 to 747 of the Criminal Code apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to a sentence of impris脗颅onment for life that is imposed under this Act, and for that purpose

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reference in sections 745.2 to 745.3 and 745.32 of the Criminal Code to a jury shall be read as a reference to the panel of a General Court Martial; and

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 226.1:

If a person is charged with murder and is provided with notice under subsection 226.01(2) or subsection 745.31(2) of the Criminal Code, the military judge presiding at the person芒聙聶s General Court Martial shall instruct the panel of the General Court Martial that, if it finds the person guilty of the murder, it must specify to the military judge, when it makes its findings,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the murder was planned and deliberate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the victim was a person described in any of paragraphs 231(4)(a) to (c) of the Criminal Code, whether the victim was such a person;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the death was caused by the person while committing or attempting to commit an offence under one of the sections referred to in any of paragraphs 231(5)(a) to (f) of the Criminal Code, whether the death was so caused;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the death was caused by the person while committing or attempting to commit a serious offence under this Act or an indictable offence under any other Act of Parliament where the act or omission constituting the offence also constituted a terrorist activity, whether the death was so caused; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if the murder was planned and deliberate and evidence was presented at the trial to establish that the person芒聙聶s behaviour, associated with the offence, was of a brutal nature, whether the behaviour was of a brutal nature.

Heading.TitleText: Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 15(2) of the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Sections 745.1 to 746.1 of the Criminal Code, other than subsection 745.21(3) and sections 745.31 and 745.32, apply, with any modifications that the circumstances require, to a sentence of life imprisonment imposed under this Act, and, for the purpose of applying those provisions,
The portion of subsection 24(1) of the International Transfer of Offenders Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Eligibility for parole芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣murder

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections 17(2) and 19(1) and subsection (1.1), if a Canadian offender was sentenced to imprisonment for life for an offence that, if it had been committed in Canada, would have constituted murder within the meaning of the Criminal Code, their full parole ineligibility period is 10 years. If, in the Minister芒聙聶s opinion, the documents supplied by the foreign entity show that the circumstances in which the offence was committed were such that, if it had been committed in Canada after July 26, 1976, it would have been first degree murder within the meaning of section 231 of that Act, the full parole ineligibility period is
Section 24 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

If a Canadian offender was sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole and, in the Minister芒聙聶s opinion, the documents supplied by the foreign entity show that the circumstances in which the offence was committed were such that, if it had been committed in Canada, it would have constituted an offence described in paragraph 744.1(1)(a) or any of subparagraphs 744.1(1)(b)(i) to (iii) of the Criminal Code, the Canadian offender is not eligible for parole.

Section.Text: Section 25 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Temporary absence and day parole芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣persons convicted of murder
Section.Text: Subject to sections 746.1 and 747 of the Criminal Code, a Canadian offender who is transferred to Canada芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣and was sentenced to imprisonment for life for an offence that, if it had been committed in Canada, would have constituted murder within the meaning of that Act芒聙聣芒聙聰芒聙聣is eligible for day parole in accordance with the Corrections and Conditional Release Act and for an absence without escort in accordance with the Corrections and Conditional Release Act or the Prisons and Reformatories Act, and their absence with escort may be authorized in accordance with the Corrections and Conditional Release Act or the Prisons and Reformatories Act. However, a Canadian offend脗颅er who was sentenced to imprisonment for life without eligibility for parole and is not eligible for parole under subsection 24(1.1) is eligible only for an absence with escort in accordance with the Corrections and Conditional Release Act or the Prisons and Reformatories Act.

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which section 17 of this Act and section 46 of An Act to enact the Canadian Victims Bill of Rights and to amend certain Acts are in force,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 156.12(1)(b) of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may disclose to the victim any information pertaining to the offender芒聙聶s correctional plan, including information regarding the offender芒聙聶s progress towards meeting the objectives of the plan, if, in the opinion of the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, the interest of the victim in the disclosure clearly outweighs any invasion of the offender芒聙聶s privacy that could result from the disclosure;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 156.12(1) of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act is amended by striking out 芒聙聹and芒聙聺 at the end of subparagraph (a)(ii), by adding 芒聙聹and芒聙聺 at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shall disclose to the victim any of the following information about the offender if, in the opinion of the Chairperson or the Commissioner, as the case may be, the disclosure would not have a negative impact on the safety of the public:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the date, if any, on which the offender is to be released on escorted temporary absence or on executive release,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any of the conditions attached to an offender芒聙聶s executive release or escorted temporary absence and the reasons for the escorted temporary absence, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the destination of the offender when released on executive release and whether the offender will be in the vicinity of the victim while travelling to that destination.

Subsection.Text: On the first day on which both subsection 49(3) of the Victims Bill of Rights and section 17 of this Act are in force, subsection 156.21(10) of the Corrections and Conditional Release Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to any conditions specified by the Board, a victim, or a person referred to in subsection 156.12(3), who does not attend a hearing that is referred to in subsection (1) as an observer is entitled, after the hearing, on request, to listen to an audio recording of the hearing, other than portions of the hearing that the Board considers could reasonably be expected to jeopardize the safety of any person or to reveal a source of information obtained in confidence.

If an observer has been present during a hearing or a victim or a person referred to in subsection 156.12(3) has exercised their right under subsection (10), any information or documents discussed or referred to during the hearing shall not for that reason alone be considered to be publicly available for purposes of the Access to Information Act or the Privacy Act.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Act, other than sections 1 and 24, come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
